statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers and its size
statement.outputFormat: The array with all contiguous duplicates removed
statement.notes: The array should be modified in-place. Time complexity: O(n).

editorial.title: Removing Contiguous Duplicates from Array
editorial.intuition: The idea is to keep adding elements to window and remove from window if duplicates are found.
editorial.bruteForce: Naive approach: Remove all duplicates from array of size n using two nested loops. Time complexity: O(n^2).
editorial.betterSolution: Use two pointers: one for adding elements and another for removing duplicates. Time complexity: O(n).
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a sliding window approach with a single pointer.

starterCode.cpp: // TODO: implement a function to remove contiguous duplicates from array
starterCode.java: // TODO: implement a function to remove contiguous duplicates from array
starterCode.javascript: // return result; // TODO: implement a function to remove contiguous duplicates from array

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void removeDuplicates(vector<int>& nums) { int n = nums.size(); for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; i++) { if (nums[i] == nums[i - 1]) nums[i] = nums[i + 1]; } } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean removeDuplicates(int[] nums) { int n = nums.length; for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; i++) { if (nums[i] == nums[i - 1]) nums[i] = nums[i + 1]; } return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def removeDuplicates(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: n = len(nums); for i in range(1, n - 1): if nums[i] == nums[i - 1]: nums[i] = nums[i + 1]; return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: class Solution { removeDuplicates(nums) { let n = nums.length; for (let i = 1; i < n - 1; i++) { if (nums[i] == nums[i - 1]) nums[i] = nums[i + 1]; } return true; } }
